

100% of reviewers gave a 5-star rating

This sentence isn’t the most persuasive, because it lacks credibility. Nobody does everything perfectly, and having 100% of reviews be 5-star reviews comes across as “fixed” or “phony”. 



90% of  reviewers gave a 5-star rating

This is already more persuasive, but credibility and trustworthiness might be still missing. We’re often doubtful when we see/hear phrases like 9 out 10… it  feels like it’s made up. Subconsciously we’re wondering: “ did you really do your homework, or are you just making up the numbers?”





89% of reviewers gave a 5-star rating

This is the most persuasive review summary out of the three options listed here. 89% feels credible and trustworthy. 



Critical Insight:



Think about this from a rational point of view. 89% is the lowest score.
